source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/06_Risk Management/250504 파생모델 추가/A-RMR-HNX_Risk Management Report (Rev.1).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB, Rev.1 dated 2025.05.04.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents risk management activities in accordance with the risk management procedure, including risk analysis, evaluation, control, residual risk review, and production/post-production information.
keyPoints: The Portable X-ray System is used for X-ray imaging and diagnosis and is not intended for mammography.
The variant model HnX-PB changed only the exterior design compared to HnX-P1; interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software are stated to be the same.
Before risk control, 6 risks were acceptable and 45 were unacceptable; after risk control, 51 risks were acceptable and 0 were unacceptable.
Overall residual risks are determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management report in accordance with the risk management procedure.

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Residual risk
concepts.evidence: residual risk risk remaining after risk control (3.21) measures have been implemented

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: Refer to A-PMS-HNX Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

concepts.name: Medical device risk management
concepts.evidence: risk management systematic application of management policies, procedures (3.13) and practices to the tasks of analysing, evaluating, controlling and monitoring risk

concepts.name: Portable X-ray system intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The device may be used for handheld diagnostic imaging of body extremities. The device may be used for stand mounted diagnostic imaging of head, abdomen, or extremities.

concepts.name: X-ray radiation protection
concepts.evidence: Operators and patients should always wear a lead apron and any other protective gear as required by law

evidence.claim: The document is a risk management report for the Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report Product name: Portable X-ray System Model name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev.1 dated 2025.05.04 and adds variant model HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 1 Rev. Date 2025.05.04 Revision Description Add variant model (HnX-PB)

evidence.claim: The report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on 2025.05.04.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Han, Jimin R&D team 2025.05.04 Reviewed by Kim, Myeongseop R&D manager 2025.05.04 Approved by Hur, Namkyu Quality Management Representative 2025.05.04

evidence.claim: Applicable national and regional regulations listed are Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017 Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The report lists EN ISO 14971:2019 as a relevant standard for application of risk management to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: The Portable X-ray System consists of an LCD display, X-ray generator, X-ray tube assembly, collimator, and cart, and can use a stand instead of a cart.
evidence.evidenceText: This Portable X-ray System (Model: HnX-P1, HnX-PB) consists of an LCD display with up and down soft-keys for controlling kV, an X-ray generator (line-powered transformer), an X-ray tube assembly, a collimator and a cart. It can also use a stand instead of a cart.

evidence.claim: The system is for X-ray imaging and diagnosis in facilities with portable or fixing sites.
evidence.evidenceText: The system is for X-ray imaging and diagnosis in facilities with portable or fixing sites.

evidence.claim: The Portable X-ray System is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: This Portable X-ray System is not intended for mammography.

evidence.claim: Model HnX-P1 and HnX-PB can be configured to an optional portable stand that complies with IEC 60601-1, with recommended maximum load of 6 kg.
evidence.evidenceText: Model HnX-P1 & HnX-PB can be configured to an optional portable stand that complies with IEC 60601- 1 safety standard. The recommended maximum load that the stand can safely carry is 6kgs

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB variant changed only the exterior design compared to HnX-P1, while interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software are the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model HnX-PB has only changed the exterior design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: No additional risk management will be conducted for addition of HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, no additional risk management will be conducted for the addition of the variant model (HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The device classification is Class llb and expected service life is approximately 7 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class llb Expected service life Approximately 7 years

evidence.claim: The intended purpose includes handheld diagnostic imaging of body extremities and stand-mounted diagnostic imaging of head, abdomen, or extremities.
evidence.evidenceText: The device may be used for handheld diagnostic imaging of body extremities. The device may be used for stand mounted diagnostic imaging of head, abdomen, or extremities.

evidence.claim: The device may be used for stand-mounted imaging of the chest when used without a grid.
evidence.evidenceText: The device may be used for stand mounted imaging of the chest when used without a grid.

evidence.claim: Limitations include not using the device on bariatric patients unless imaging body extremities and not for mammography use.
evidence.evidenceText: - Not to be used on bariatric patients, unless imaging body extremities - Not for mammography use

evidence.claim: The device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system, which may be required for full optimization of image quality and radiation exposure for different exam types.

evidence.claim: The device users include surgeon, physician, radiographer or radiology technician, and other medical staff.
evidence.evidenceText: Use and maintenance User(s): Surgeon; physician; radiographer or radiology technician; other medical staff

evidence.claim: Reasonably foreseeable misuse may include maintenance and calibration error of operator.
evidence.evidenceText: Reasonably foreseeable misuse Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

evidence.claim: The product contains software.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.19 Does the medical device contain software? A The product contains software.

evidence.claim: The Portable X-ray System can be connected to a PC via Bluetooth.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.20 Does the medical device allow access to information? A Portable X-ray System can be connected to the PC via Bluetooth

evidence.claim: The product does not store data.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.21 Does the medical device store data critical to patient care? N/A This product does not store data.

evidence.claim: The system has a rechargeable battery and adapter.
evidence.evidenceText: 7.9.2.4 Electrical power source A Portable X-ray System has the rechargeable battery and adapter.

evidence.claim: Battery specification includes 29.6V/1,500mAh Li-Polymer cell, 25.6~33.6VDC output voltage, 50A output current at 2 seconds, BMS control circuit, and 2 hour charging time.
evidence.evidenceText: Battery spec is; - Cell : 29.6V/1,500mAh, Li-Polymer - Output Voltage : 25.6~33.6VDC - Output Current : 50A @ (2sec) - Control Circuit : BMS - Charging Time : 2hr

evidence.claim: The maximum output of the X-ray generator is 720 W, tube output voltage is 50~90 kV, and tube output current is 10 mA at 50~70 kV or 8 mA at 71~90 kV.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum output of X-ray generator is 720W The X-ray Tube output voltage of generator is 50~90Kv The X-ray Tube output current and mAs ... 50~70 10 0.4~20 71~90 8 0.4~16

evidence.claim: Before risk control, the risk evaluation results were 6 acceptable, 45 unacceptable, total 51.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 2. Risk evaluation results before risk control ... EN ISO 14971:2019 (&IEC 60601-1) 6 45 51

evidence.claim: The text states that 43 risks were identified and estimated, while nearby tables total 51 risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated. ... Acceptable region: 6ea - Unacceptable region: 45ea

evidence.claim: After risk control, all identified residual risks were distributed in an acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

evidence.claim: After risk control, risk evaluation results were 51 acceptable, 0 unacceptable, total 51.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk evaluation results after risk control (Numbers in the regions) ... EN ISO 14971:2019 51 0 51

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information collection includes customer satisfaction surveys, customer complaint statistics, inspection and test monitoring, measuring device monitoring, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, process monitoring, and purchaser assessment results.
evidence.evidenceText: Production and post-production information Collection and review activities in production and post-production phase Department Information collecting and review in production and post-production Quality Assurance 1. Customer satisfaction survey ... Management Monitoring the purchaser assessment results

evidence.claim: The Quality manager shall report safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency if necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text does not identify FDA as an applicable authority; the text lists European Union, Republic of Korea, FCC SUB PART C, and EU Radio Equipment Directive references.
Risk count evidence is conflicting: the analysis states 43 risks were identified and estimated, while the before-control table and counts show 6 acceptable plus 45 unacceptable, total 51.
OCR or transcription quality issues are present, including apparent typos such as 'Class llb', 'EN 15322-1' where symbol standard context otherwise lists EN 15223-1, and spacing/word errors.
